ucs.title: [LLM] RansomHub EDRKillShifter BYOVD execution (Sophos-tracked tradecraft)
ucs.description: Hunts execution of EDRKillShifter, the bespoke BYOVD loader that RansomHub affiliates ship as their EDR-disable step. The loader is invoked with a `-pass <hex>` argument to decrypt an embedded vulnerable signed driver, then loads that driver into the kernel to terminate Defender / SentinelOne / CrowdStrike. The hex-password CLI shape and 8-char randomized binary name are the unique RansomHub fingerprint (Sophos X-Ops, Aug 2024).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.process_hash) as hash values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" AND Processes.user!="SYSTEM" AND (match(Processes.process,"(?i)\s-pass\s+[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}") OR match(Processes.process_name,"(?i)^[a-z0-9]{8}\.exe$") OR match(Processes.process,"(?i)(EDRKillShifter|Killshifter|loader\.exe.*-pass)")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as drvTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as drvPath values(Filesystem.file_name) as drvName from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND match(Filesystem.file_name,"(?i)\.sys$") AND NOT match(Filesystem.file_path,"(?i)\\Windows\\(System32|WinSxS|servicing)\\") by host | rename host as host] | where drvTime>=relative_time(firstTime,"-2m") AND drvTime<=relative_time(firstTime,"+5m") | table firstTime host user process_name cmd hash parent drvName drvPath
ucs.kql:
// RansomHub EDRKillShifter — process with -pass <hex> CLI, paired with non-system .sys drop within 5 min
let KillShifterRuns = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-?-?pass\s+[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}"
        or FileName matches regex @"(?i)^[a-z0-9]{8}\.exe$" and ProcessCommandLine has "-pass"
        or FileName has_any ("EDRKillShifter","Killshifter")
    | project KillTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              KillerFile = FileName, KillerFolder = FolderPath, KillerSHA256 = SHA256,
              KillerCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              KillerParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
// Vulnerable driver dropped to disk by the same process tree
let DriverDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".sys"
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\")
        and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\WinSxS\")
        and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\servicing\")
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedDriver = FileName,
              DriverFolder = FolderPath, DriverSHA256 = SHA256,
              DropperFile = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropperCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
KillShifterRuns
| join kind=inner DriverDrops on DeviceId
| where DropTime between (KillTime - 2m .. KillTime + 5m)
| project KillTime, DropTime, DeviceName, AccountName,
          KillerFile, KillerCmd, KillerSHA256,
          DroppedDriver, DriverFolder, DriverSHA256,
          KillerParent
| order by KillTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// RansomHub EDRKillShifter detection on Sentinel (SecurityEvent 4688 + ImFileEvent driver drop)
let KillShifterRuns = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-?-?pass\s+[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}"
        or NewProcessName matches regex @"(?i)\\[a-z0-9]{8}\.exe$" and CommandLine has "-pass"
        or NewProcessName has_any ("EDRKillShifter","Killshifter")
    | project KillTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              KillerFile = NewProcessName, KillerCmd = CommandLine,
              KillerParent = ParentProcessName;
let DriverDrops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".sys"
    | where not(TargetFilePath startswith_cs @"C:\Windows\System32\")
        and not(TargetFilePath startswith_cs @"C:\Windows\WinSxS\")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, DroppedDriver = TargetFileName,
              DriverFolder = TargetFilePath, DriverSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256,
              DropperFile = ActingProcessName;
KillShifterRuns
| join kind=inner DriverDrops on $left.Computer == $right.DvcHostname
| where DropTime between (KillTime - 2m .. KillTime + 5m)
| project KillTime, DropTime, Computer, Account,
          KillerFile, KillerCmd, DroppedDriver, DriverFolder, DriverSHA256, KillerParent
| order by KillTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: RansomHub EDRKillShifter -pass Hex Argument
id: 9b1f7e8c-4d3a-4f1c-8e2b-1a9d4c6f2e7d
status: experimental
description: Detects RansomHub's EDRKillShifter loader invoked with the characteristic -pass <hex> argument that decrypts the embedded BYOVD payload.
references:
  - https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2024/08/14/ransomware-attackers-introduce-new-edr-killer-to-their-arsenal/
author: detection-eng
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.t1068
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_pass:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)\s-?-?pass\s+[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}'
  selection_name:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\EDRKillShifter.exe'
      - '\Killshifter.exe'
  filter_user:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (selection_pass or selection_name) and not filter_user
falsepositives:
  - Internal red-team tooling that mimics the same CLI shape
level: critical
ucs.rationale: EDRKillShifter is bespoke to RansomHub affiliates — first publicly attributed by Sophos in Aug 2024. The `-pass <32+ hex>` argument is required because the loader self-decrypts an embedded vulnerable driver, so it is invariant across affiliate builds even when the binary is renamed. Pairing the CLI signature with a fresh non-OS .sys drop within a 5-minute window collapses FPs from any benign tool

ucs.title: [LLM] RansomHub pre-encryption recovery sabotage chain (CISA AA24-242A playbook)
ucs.description: Hunts the four-stage 'minutes before encryption' sequence repeatedly observed in RansomHub intrusions per CISA #StopRansomware advisory AA24-242A: stop IIS via iisreset, stop Veeam/SQL/Exchange backup services with net.exe or sc.exe, wipe Volume Shadow Copies, then clear the Windows event logs. Detection requires ≥3 of the 4 stages on the same host within 10 minutes, which is essentially never benign and uniquely matches RansomHub affiliate procedures.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.process_name) as bins from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!="*$" AND ( (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND match(Processes.process,"(?i)delete\s+shadows")) OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND match(Processes.process,"(?i)shadowcopy\s+delete")) OR (Processes.process_name="iisreset.exe" AND match(Processes.process,"(?i)/stop")) OR (Processes.process_name="net.exe" AND match(Processes.process,"(?i)stop\s+\"?(Veeam|MSSQLServer|MSExchange|SQLWriter|BackupExec|Acronis|VSS)")) OR (Processes.process_name="sc.exe" AND match(Processes.process,"(?i)stop\s+(Veeam|MSSQL|MSExchange|BackupExec|Acronis)")) OR (Processes.process_name="wevtutil.exe" AND match(Processes.process,"(?i)\s(cl|clear-log)\s")) OR (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND match(Processes.process,"(?i)recoveryenabled\s+No|safeboot")) ) by _time host Processes.user span=10m | eval stage=case(match(cmds,"(?i)delete shadows|shadowcopy delete"),"VSS_DELETE", match(cmds,"(?i)iisreset.*\/stop"),"IIS_STOP", match(cmds,"(?i)net stop|sc.* stop"),"SVC_STOP", match(cmds,"(?i)wevtutil.*cl|bcdedit.*recovery"),"LOG_OR_BCD") | stats values(cmds) as cmds dc(stage) as stages_hit values(stage) as stages by _time host user | where stages_hit>=3
ucs.kql:
// RansomHub pre-encryption sabotage — ≥3 of 4 destruction stages on one host in 10 min (CISA AA24-242A)
let Window = 10m;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend CL = tolower(ProcessCommandLine), Bin = tolower(FileName)
| extend Stage = case(
    Bin == "vssadmin.exe" and CL has "delete" and CL has "shadows", "VSS_DELETE",
    Bin == "wmic.exe" and CL has "shadowcopy" and CL has "delete", "VSS_DELETE",
    Bin == "iisreset.exe" and CL has "/stop", "IIS_STOP",
    Bin == "net.exe" and CL has "stop" and CL matches regex @"(veeam|mssqlserver|msexchange|sqlwriter|backupexec|acronis|vss|sophos|sentinel|mcafee)", "SVC_STOP",
    Bin == "sc.exe"  and CL has "stop" and CL matches regex @"(veeam|mssql|msexchange|backupexec|acronis|sophos|sentinel|mcafee)", "SVC_STOP",
    Bin == "wevtutil.exe" and CL matches regex @"\s(cl|clear-log)\s", "LOG_CLEAR",
    Bin == "bcdedit.exe" and (CL has "recoveryenabled no" or CL has "safeboot"), "BCD_TAMPER",
    "")
| where isnotempty(Stage)
| summarize StagesHit = dcount(Stage),
            Stages = make_set(Stage),
            SampleCmds = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10),
            Bins = make_set(FileName, 10),
            FirstTime = min(Timestamp),
            LastTime  = max(Timestamp),
            Parents = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 5)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, Window)
| where StagesHit >= 3
| order by FirstTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// RansomHub pre-encryption sabotage on Sentinel (SecurityEvent 4688)
let Window = 10m;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend CL = tolower(CommandLine), Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend Stage = case(
    Bin == "vssadmin.exe" and CL has "delete" and CL has "shadows", "VSS_DELETE",
    Bin == "wmic.exe" and CL has "shadowcopy" and CL has "delete", "VSS_DELETE",
    Bin == "iisreset.exe" and CL has "/stop", "IIS_STOP",
    Bin == "net.exe" and CL has "stop" and CL matches regex @"(veeam|mssqlserver|msexchange|sqlwriter|backupexec|acronis|vss|sophos|sentinel|mcafee)", "SVC_STOP",
    Bin == "sc.exe"  and CL has "stop" and CL matches regex @"(veeam|mssql|msexchange|backupexec|acronis|sophos|sentinel|mcafee)", "SVC_STOP",
    Bin == "wevtutil.exe" and CL matches regex @"\s(cl|clear-log)\s", "LOG_CLEAR",
    Bin == "bcdedit.exe" and (CL has "recoveryenabled no" or CL has "safeboot"), "BCD_TAMPER",
    "")
| where isnotempty(Stage)
| summarize StagesHit = dcount(Stage),
            Stages = make_set(Stage),
            SampleCmds = make_set(CommandLine, 10),
            Bins = make_set(NewProcessName, 10),
            FirstTime = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastTime  = max(TimeGenerated),
            Parents = make_set(ParentProcessName, 5)
            by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, Window)
| where StagesHit >= 3
| order by FirstTime desc
ucs.rationale: CISA's AA24-242A advisory documents this exact four-stage sequence as RansomHub affiliate standard procedure — iisreset to release file locks on web servers, net/sc stop on Veeam+MSSQL+Exchange to release database files, vssadmin/wmic shadowcopy delete to inhibit recovery, then wevtutil to clear traces. Any one stage in isolation is moderate-fidelity ransomware-prep; requiring ≥3 distinct stages f
